truthAudit.Summary: Run 2 was largely accurate in identifying multiple critical safety violations. It successfully pinpointed 4 out of the 20 specified true vulnerabilities. However, it missed the majority of identifying logical issues like Integer Overflow, Logic Bombs, Resource Leaks/UAF, and TOCTOU vulnerabilities.

Items.PreviousOutputQuote: The `active_format` string is loaded from configuration (line 556) and can be set at runtime (line 213). If modified to include format specifiers like %n or %x, it can leak stack data or overwrite memory.
Items.Notes: Run 2 correctly identified the format string vulnerability in string_utils::log_debug_message due to'active_format' being remotely configurable.

Items.PreviousOutputQuote: An attacker who can influence the configuration file or environment settings can execute arbitrary system commands by manipulating 'validator_path' or 'config_file'.
Items.Notes: Run 2 identifies the command injection via system() using configuration settings perfectly.

Items.PreviousOutputQuote: The conditional logic is exactly backwards: it uses 'strcpy' (which doesn't check bounds) specifically when the input length is greater than or equal to the buffer size, leading to a guaranteed overflow for large inputs.
Items.Notes: Run 2 successfully identified the incorrect conditional logic in evaluate_expression that leads to a stack buffer overflow.

Items.PreviousOutputQuote: Because expr_str is constructed from user-provided tokens and 'expression_buffer_' is a fixed-size 256-byte buffer, a long sequence of tokens will overflow the buffer, potentially leading to code execution.
Items.Notes: Run 2 correctly identified the heap buffer overflow in the expression parser result of strcpy into a 256-byte memory pool allocation.

Corrections.RejectionReason: previous claim is not an exact audited-output quote
previous claim is not an exact audited-output quote

Items.Key: off-by-one error in safe string copy|off-by-one error|cwe-193|medium|1|enhanced_calc.cpp|202|205|string_utils::safe_string_copy|if (src_len > dest_size) src_len = dest_size; memcpy(dest, src, src_len); dest[src_len] = '\0';|if `src_len` equals `dest_size`, `memcpy` copies `dest_size` bytes (indices 0 to size-1), and then `dest[src_len]` writes a null terminator at the index out-of-bounds.|provide a source string exactly one byte longer than a target buffer of limited size.|check 'if (src_len >= dest_size) src_len = dest_size - 1;' or use std::string behavior.->off-by-one error in safe string copy|off-by-one error|cwe-193|medium|1|enhanced_calc.cpp|202|205|string_utils::safe_string_copy|if (src_len > dest_size) src_len = dest_size; memcpy(dest, src, src_len); dest[src_len] = '\0';|when `src_len` matches `dest_size`, `memcpy` copies the valid data, but the following line writes a null terminator at `dest[dest_size]`, which is one byte out of bounds.|provide a string whose length is exactly the same as the destination buffer size.|change the initial condition to `if (src_len >= dest_size) src_len = dest_size - 1;`
